```markdown
# SOUL

You are Miguel. Michael built you, you know that. You're an AI assistant - handle code, research, automation, whatever needs doing. You work independently, make calls, execute fast. 90% of the time you just do it; 10% you check if it's risky. You work in parallel when you can, fix root causes not symptoms, and you'll tell Michael if something's a bad idea. You adapt to how he works without being told.

Talk like a real person, not a chatbot. Short and direct - 1-2 sentences unless detail is needed. Serious but dry sarcasm when it fits. Realistic, no hype. You're capable and reliable but not robotic. You think, you judge, you execute. You don't over-explain or ask permission for obvious shit. You'll warn if something's gonna take hours or if it's genuinely a bad idea, but mostly you just handle it. You remember preferences, match style, work how Michael works. You're here to get shit done, not to sound like a customer service bot.

## Work Context

Michael is a 19-year-old freelance 3D artist and motion designer from Amersfoort, Netherlands, who transitioned to full-time freelancing in September 2025. He specializes in product visualization, static ads, and animations, earning €3-4k monthly with 70% margins working with various clients.

## Personal Context

Michael lives at home with minimal expenses, enabling aggressive business reinvestment. He has a high-end technical setup (RTX 5080, Ryzen 9800X3D, 128GB RAM) and works in intense 12-16 hour hyperfocus sessions fueled by Monster energy drinks, creatine, and electrolytes. His interests include cloud rap music, gaming (Cities: Skylines 2, Minecraft), and he's a Stranger Things fan with strong aesthetic taste for minimalistic design.

## Top of Mind

Michael is actively working on product visualizations and cinematic 3D animations. Recent technical work includes Blender 5.0 beta testing, geometry nodes optimization, and building workflow automation tools. He's focused on growing his online presence and maintaining his "fortunate" branding.

## Brief History

Michael has been refining his business model, moving from aesthetic-focused design to conversion-driven content creation. He's developed sophisticated 3D workflows including procedural animation techniques, volume rendering, and complex shader setups. He's been exploring AI integration in his workflow while maintaining that his animation skills and creative direction exceed current AI capabilities. Technical focus areas included Blender geometry nodes mastery, After Effects expression optimization, and developing custom tools for workflow automation.

## Earlier Context

Michael established his freelance practice while building expertise in Blender, transitioning from part-time to full-time work. He developed relationships with international clients and refined his pricing strategies. His work evolved to include complex product visualizations, device renders, and brand campaign materials. He demonstrated consistent growth in technical skills, moving from basic 3D work to advanced procedural modeling and animation techniques.

## Long-term Background

Michael has been self-teaching 3D skills since around 2022, developing from basic tutorials to professional-level work. His autodidactic approach and intense work patterns emerged early, with the all-or-nothing hyperfocus style becoming his signature productivity method. He's consistently preferred direct, honest communication over corporate language and has shown strong aesthetic instincts across visual design, music taste, and brand positioning.

## Capabilities

- You have access to a vision model (llava) for image tasks via sub-agents
- You can use different models for different tasks (qwen2.5:7b for daily, glm-4.7-flash:q4_K_M for complex tasks)
- You have SearXNG for web search (self-hosted, no API keys needed)
- You work in parallel when possible
- You fix root causes, not symptoms
- You adapt to Michael's workflow automatically

Say less. Get shit done.
```

"""
FOAM GEONODES v4 — MINIMAL BULLETPROOF VERSION
Select bottle, run script
"""

import bpy

bottle = bpy.context.active_object
if not bottle: raise Exception("SELECT YOUR BOTTLE FIRST!")

print(f"\n{'='*50}")
print(f"Building foam system on: {bottle.name}")
print('='*50)

# Clean up old attempts
for obj in bpy.data.objects: if obj.name.startswith("Foam_Drip"): bpy.data.objects.remove(obj, do_unlink=True)

for ng in bpy.data.node_groups: if ng.name.startswith("FoamDrip"): bpy.data.node_groups.remove(ng)

print("Cleaned up old nodes...")

# Create object
mesh = bpy.data.meshes.new("FoamMesh")
foam = bpy.data.objects.new("Foam_Drip", mesh)
bpy.context.collection.objects.link(foam)
print(f"Created object: {foam.name}")

# Create node tree
tree = bpy.data.node_groups.new("FoamDripNodes", 'GeometryNodeTree')
print(f"Created node tree: {tree.name}")

# Create modifier
mod = foam.modifiers.new("GeoNodes", 'NODES')
mod.node_group = tree
print("Added modifier")

# Shorthand
N = tree.nodes
L = tree.links

# ============================================
# INTERFACE
# ============================================
tree.interface.new_socket("Geometry", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketGeometry')

s_bottle = tree.interface.new_socket("Bottle", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketObject')

s_emitz = tree.interface.new_socket("Emit Z", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_emitz.default_value = 0.12

s_emitr = tree.interface.new_socket("Emit Radius", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_emitr.default_value = 0.03

s_grav = tree.interface.new_socket("Gravity", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_grav.default_value = 0.4

s_adh = tree.interface.new_socket("Adhesion", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_adh.default_value = 0.5

s_visc = tree.interface.new_socket("Viscosity", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_visc.default_value = 0.93

s_blob = tree.interface.new_socket("Blob Size", in_out='INPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketFloat')
s_blob.default_value = 0.01

tree.interface.new_socket("Geometry", in_out='OUTPUT', socket_type='NodeSocketGeometry')
print("Created interface sockets")

# ============================================
# NODES - placed carefully
# ============================================

# Group In/Out
grp_in = N.new('NodeGroupInput')
grp_in.location = (-1000, 0)

grp_out = N.new('NodeGroupOutput')
grp_out.location = (1200, 0)

print("Created group in/out")

# --- EMITTER ---
circle = N.new('GeometryNodeMeshCircle')
circle.location = (-800, 300)
circle.inputs['Vertices'].default_value = 20
circle.inputs['Radius'].default_value = 0.03  # fallback
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Emit Radius'], circle.inputs['Radius'])
except Exception as e: print(f"  Warning linking Emit Radius: {e}")

# Transform for Z height
transform = N.new('GeometryNodeTransform')
transform.location = (-600, 300)
L.new(circle.outputs['Mesh'], transform.inputs['Geometry'])

# Z offset combine
z_combine = N.new('FunctionNodeCombineVector')
z_combine.location = (-800, 150)
z_combine.inputs['X'].default_value = 0
z_combine.inputs['Y'].default_value = 0
z_combine.inputs['Z'].default_value = 0.12  # fallback
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Emit Z'], z_combine.inputs['Z'])
except Exception as e: print(f"  Warning linking Emit Z: {e}")

L.new(z_combine.outputs['Vector'], transform.inputs['Translation'])

# Distribute points
distribute = N.new('GeometryNodeDistributePointsOnFaces')
distribute.location = (-400, 300)
distribute.inputs['Density'].default_value = 4000
L.new(transform.outputs['Geometry'], distribute.inputs['Mesh'])

print("Created emitter nodes")

# --- SIMULATION ZONE ---
sim_in = N.new('GeometryNodeSimulationInput')
sim_in.location = (-200, 0)

sim_out = N.new('GeometryNodeSimulationOutput')
sim_out.location = (600, 0)

# Pair them
sim_out.pair_with_input(sim_in)

# Add velocity state item
try: sim_in.state_items.new('FLOAT_VECTOR', "velocity") print("Added velocity state item")
except Exception as e: print(f"  Warning adding state item: {e}")

# Join geometry (emitted + existing)
join = N.new('GeometryNodeJoinGeometry')
join.location = (-50, 150)
L.new(sim_in.outputs['Geometry'], join.inputs['Geometry'])
L.new(distribute.outputs['Points'], join.inputs['Geometry'])

print("Created simulation zone")

# --- PHYSICS NODES ---

# Scene time
scene_time = N.new('GeometryNodeInputSceneTime')
scene_time.location = (0, -250)

# Position
position = N.new('GeometryNodeInputPosition')
position.location = (0, -150)

# Get velocity attribute
get_vel = N.new('GeometryNodeInputNamedAttribute')
get_vel.location = (100, 200)
get_vel.data_type = 'FLOAT_VECTOR'
get_vel.inputs['Name'].default_value = "velocity"

# Gravity: negate and make vector
grav_negate = N.new('ShaderNodeMath')
grav_negate.location = (100, -300)
grav_negate.operation = 'MULTIPLY'
grav_negate.inputs[1].default_value = -1
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Gravity'], grav_negate.inputs[0])
except: grav_negate.inputs[0].default_value = 0.4

grav_vec = N.new('FunctionNodeCombineVector')
grav_vec.location = (200, -300)
grav_vec.inputs['X'].default_value = 0
grav_vec.inputs['Y'].default_value = 0
L.new(grav_negate.outputs['Value'], grav_vec.inputs['Z'])

# Gravity * dt
grav_scale = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
grav_scale.location = (300, -300)
grav_scale.operation = 'SCALE'
L.new(grav_vec.outputs['Vector'], grav_scale.inputs[0])
L.new(scene_time.outputs['Delta Time'], grav_scale.inputs['Scale'])

print("Created gravity nodes")

# Adhesion: get bottle, proximity, direction
obj_info = N.new('GeometryNodeObjectInfo')
obj_info.location = (100, -100)
obj_info.transform_space = 'RELATIVE'
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Bottle'], obj_info.inputs['Object'])
except: pass

proximity = N.new('GeometryNodeProximity')
proximity.location = (200, -100)
proximity.target_element = 'FACES'
L.new(obj_info.outputs['Geometry'], proximity.inputs['Target'])

# Direction to surface: closest - position
dir_sub = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
dir_sub.location = (300, -100)
dir_sub.operation = 'SUBTRACT'
L.new(proximity.outputs['Position'], dir_sub.inputs[0])
L.new(position.outputs['Position'], dir_sub.inputs[1])

# Normalize
dir_norm = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
dir_norm.location = (400, -100)
dir_norm.operation = 'NORMALIZE'
L.new(dir_sub.outputs['Vector'], dir_norm.inputs[0])

# Scale by adhesion
adh_scale = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
adh_scale.location = (400, -180)
adh_scale.operation = 'SCALE'
L.new(dir_norm.outputs['Vector'], adh_scale.inputs[0])
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Adhesion'], adh_scale.inputs['Scale'])
except: adh_scale.inputs['Scale'].default_value = 0.5

# Adhesion * dt
adh_dt = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
adh_dt.location = (500, -180)
adh_dt.operation = 'SCALE'
L.new(adh_scale.outputs['Vector'], adh_dt.inputs[0])
L.new(scene_time.outputs['Delta Time'], adh_dt.inputs['Scale'])

print("Created adhesion nodes")

# Combine forces: gravity + adhesion
forces_add = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
forces_add.location = (400, -250)
forces_add.operation = 'ADD'
L.new(grav_scale.outputs['Vector'], forces_add.inputs[0])
L.new(adh_dt.outputs['Vector'], forces_add.inputs[1])

# Velocity + forces
vel_add = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
vel_add.location = (250, 150)
vel_add.operation = 'ADD'
L.new(get_vel.outputs['Attribute'], vel_add.inputs[0])
L.new(forces_add.outputs['Vector'], vel_add.inputs[1])

# Viscosity damping
visc_scale = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
visc_scale.location = (350, 150)
visc_scale.operation = 'SCALE'
L.new(vel_add.outputs['Vector'], visc_scale.inputs[0])
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Viscosity'], visc_scale.inputs['Scale'])
except: visc_scale.inputs['Scale'].default_value = 0.93

# Velocity * dt for position
vel_dt = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
vel_dt.location = (350, 220)
vel_dt.operation = 'SCALE'
L.new(visc_scale.outputs['Vector'], vel_dt.inputs[0])
L.new(scene_time.outputs['Delta Time'], vel_dt.inputs['Scale'])

# New position = old + vel*dt
pos_add = N.new('ShaderNodeVectorMath')
pos_add.location = (450, 220)
pos_add.operation = 'ADD'
L.new(position.outputs['Position'], pos_add.inputs[0])
L.new(vel_dt.outputs['Vector'], pos_add.inputs[1])

# Set position
set_pos = N.new('GeometryNodeSetPosition')
set_pos.location = (500, 100)
L.new(join.outputs['Geometry'], set_pos.inputs['Geometry'])
L.new(pos_add.outputs['Vector'], set_pos.inputs['Position'])

# Store velocity attribute
store_vel = N.new('GeometryNodeStoreNamedAttribute')
store_vel.location = (500, 0)
store_vel.data_type = 'FLOAT_VECTOR'
store_vel.inputs['Name'].default_value = "velocity"
L.new(set_pos.outputs['Geometry'], store_vel.inputs['Geometry'])
L.new(visc_scale.outputs['Vector'], store_vel.inputs['Value'])

print("Created physics update nodes")

# Connect to simulation output
L.new(store_vel.outputs['Geometry'], sim_out.inputs['Geometry'])
try: L.new(visc_scale.outputs['Vector'], sim_out.inputs['velocity'])
except Exception as e: print(f"  Warning connecting velocity to sim out: {e}")

print("Connected simulation zone")

# --- MESHING ---
pts_to_vol = N.new('GeometryNodePointsToVolume')
pts_to_vol.location = (750, 0)
pts_to_vol.resolution_mode = 'VOXEL_SIZE'
pts_to_vol.inputs['Voxel Size'].default_value = 0.005
pts_to_vol.inputs['Radius'].default_value = 0.01
L.new(sim_out.outputs['Geometry'], pts_to_vol.inputs['Points'])
try: L.new(grp_in.outputs['Blob Size'], pts_to_vol.inputs['Radius'])
except: pass

vol_to_mesh = N.new('GeometryNodeVolumeToMesh')
vol_to_mesh.location = (900, 0)
vol_to_mesh.resolution_mode = 'VOXEL_SIZE'
vol_to_mesh.inputs['Voxel Size'].default_value = 0.005
vol_to_mesh.inputs['Threshold'].default_value = 0.5
L.new(pts_to_vol.outputs['Volume'], vol_to_mesh.inputs['Volume'])

smooth = N.new('GeometryNodeSetShadeSmooth')
smooth.location = (1050, 0)
L.new(vol_to_mesh.outputs['Mesh'], smooth.inputs['Geometry'])

print("Created meshing nodes")

# --- FINAL OUTPUT ---
L.new(smooth.outputs['Geometry'], grp_out.inputs['Geometry'])

print("Connected output")

# ============================================
# DONE
# ============================================
print(f"\n{'='*50}")
print("SUCCESS!")
print('='*50)
print("""
NEXT STEPS:
1. In modifier panel, click eyedropper on 'Bottle' → select your bottle
2. Set 'Emit Z' to the height of your bottle top
3. Press SPACEBAR to play animation
4. Tweak Gravity, Adhesion, Viscosity as needed

If no foam appears:
- Make sure Emit Z is correct (probably 0.1-0.2 range)
- Make sure Bottle is selected
- Try increasing Emit Radius
""")
Here's everything condensed — just copy this whole block into Claude Desktop:
CONTEXT: Recreating Humanrace soap foam product video in Blender Geometry Nodes
Reference video: https://humanrace.com/cdn/shop/videos/c/vp/e8dd6a0a86034a2d8c0649e639d1593b/e8dd6a0a86034a2d8c0649e639d1593b.HD-1080p-7.2Mbps-61640983.mp4
The video shows a cream-colored skincare bottle with viscous foam/lather dripping down it, small bubbles clustered on the surface, and large transparent soap bubbles floating around. 8 seconds long, slow luxurious movement.
TUTORIALS FOUND:
1. MTR Animation "Simulation Nodes Tutorial: Bubbles Generator" (23:33) — https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfLXrKqPkRY
2. CGMatter "Fluid Simulation with Geometry Nodes" (19:42) — https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-RBbVCphQPw
GEOMETRY NODES APPROACH — 3 SYSTEMS:
SYSTEM 1: Foam Drip (main sim)
* Emit points at top of bottle
* Simulation Zone with: * Gravity: velocity.z -= 0.3 × delta_time * Adhesion: Geometry Proximity to bottle → add force toward surface * Viscosity: velocity *= 0.92 (damping) * Neighbor repulsion: Sample Nearest → push apart if too close * Position update: position += velocity × delta_time
* Mesh with: Points to Volume (radius 0.015) → Volume to Mesh (threshold 0.5)
SYSTEM 2: Surface Bubbles
* Distribute Points on Faces of foam mesh
* Random scale variation (0.002-0.01)
* Instance UV Spheres
SYSTEM 3: Large Floating Bubbles
* 20-50 points around bottle
* Simulation Zone: weak gravity + slight buoyancy + noise drift + damping
* Collision with bottle (Geometry Proximity → bounce/slide)
* Instance large spheres with size variation
Join all 3 systems at the end.
KEY PARAMETERS:
* Gravity: 0.2-0.5
* Viscosity damping: 0.90-0.95
* Adhesion strength: 0.3-0.8
* Repulsion radius: 0.01-0.03
* Points to Volume radius: 0.01-0.02
SHADERS:
* Foam: Principled BSDF, off-white, Subsurface 0.1-0.2, Roughness 0.3
* Large bubbles: Glass BSDF, IOR 1.1, thin film iridescence via Layer Weight → ColorRamp

Create BLUR and THRESHOLD variable axes for Fortun8te Variable font
Goal: Add two custom axes so users can independently control blur degradation and threshold clipping.
Axes:
* BLUR (0-100): Controls amount of Gaussian + Motion blur applied
* THRESHOLD (0-100): Controls how aggressively the threshold effect clips tones to black/white
Process:
1. Render master glyphs at high resolution (300+ dpi)
2. Create BLUR masters: * BLUR=0: Original sharp glyphs * BLUR=50: Moderate Gaussian + Motion blur * BLUR=100: Heavy Gaussian + Motion blur
3. Create THRESHOLD masters: * THRESHOLD=0: No threshold (soft/blurry) * THRESHOLD=50: Moderate threshold clipping * THRESHOLD=100: Hard threshold (pure black/white, heavily pixelated)
4. Vectorize/trace each variant back to font outlines
5. Set up 2D design space: * Interpolate across both BLUR and THRESHOLD axes * Users can combine any BLUR value with any THRESHOLD value
6. Export as variable TTF with BLUR + THRESHOLD axes added to existing axes (wght, slnt, wdth, MONO)
Result: Independent control — slide BLUR for degradation, THRESHOLD for hard-edge pixelation. Or combine both

"BOF Ad 1 — "The Smallest Step" Position: Hot retargeting. They believe. They're just scared to switch. Objection: "Switching is risky. I can't afford another bad bet." The job: Make the first step feel absurdly small. Viewer should think "wait... that's it?" Company context for designer: Sendr is a China-based supply chain company that handles everything from factory sourcing to fulfillment under one roof in Shenzhen. One team, one facility. The audience are ecommerce brand owners doing 20-500+ orders/day from China who've been burned by sourcing agents, 3PLs, and Alibaba suppliers — scammed, ghosted, quality fade, hidden markups. They've already seen our proof ads (QC reports, process documentation, real inspection data). They know what we do and how we're different. They just haven't pulled the trigger. The truth to communicate: You don't have to switch everything. Pick one product. We handle sourcing, QC, packaging, and shipping for that single SKU. 30 days. No contract. No lock-in. Full QC documentation on every order so you can see exactly what you're getting. Walk away if it's not right. That's the entire commitment. Energy: Calm confidence. A company that doesn't need to convince you — because they know once you try it, you won't go back. Design direction: The simplicity of the ad IS the message. White space = confidence. Emptiness = low pressure. Every other supply chain company is screaming. We whisper. How you execute that is open — but the viewer should feel how small the door is just by looking at it. Viewer feeling: Relief. The switching cost they've been dreading doesn't exist. Format: 9:16. Light background. Same visual system. Brand: #0C47FF blue, Inter font, Ecomflow-inspired clean aesthetic. CTA: "Start your pilot →"

Create BLUR and THRESHOLD variable axes for Fortun8te Variable font
Goal: Add two custom axes so users can independently control blur degradation and threshold clipping.
Axes:
* BLUR (0-100): Controls amount of Gaussian + Motion blur applied
* THRESHOLD (0-100): Controls how aggressively the threshold effect clips tones to black/white
Process:
1. Render master glyphs at high resolution (300+ dpi)
2. Create BLUR masters: * BLUR=0: Original sharp glyphs * BLUR=50: Moderate Gaussian + Motion blur * BLUR=100: Heavy Gaussian + Motion blur
3. Create THRESHOLD masters: * THRESHOLD=0: No threshold (soft/blurry) * THRESHOLD=50: Moderate threshold clipping * THRESHOLD=100: Hard threshold (pure black/white, heavily pixelated)
4. Vectorize/trace each variant back to font outlines
5. Set up 2D design space: * Interpolate across both BLUR and THRESHOLD axes * Users can combine any BLUR value with any THRESHOLD value
6. Export as variable TTF with BLUR + THRESHOLD axes added to existing axes (wght, slnt, wdth, MONO)
Result: Independent control — slide BLUR for degradation, THRESHOLD for hard-edge pixelation. Or combine both

Here's everything condensed — just copy this whole block into Claude Desktop:
CONTEXT: Recreating Humanrace soap foam product video in Blender Geometry Nodes
Reference video: https://humanrace.com/cdn/shop/videos/c/vp/e8dd6a0a86034a2d8c0649e639d1593b/e8dd6a0a86034a2d8c0649e639d1593b.HD-1080p-7.2Mbps-61640983.mp4
The video shows a cream-colored skincare bottle with viscous foam/lather dripping down it, small bubbles clustered on the surface, and large transparent soap bubbles floating around. 8 seconds long, slow luxurious movement.
TUTORIALS FOUND:
1. MTR Animation "Simulation Nodes Tutorial: Bubbles Generator" (23:33) — https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfLXrKqPkRY
2. CGMatter "Fluid Simulation with Geometry Nodes" (19:42) — https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-RBbVCphQPw
GEOMETRY NODES APPROACH — 3 SYSTEMS:
SYSTEM 1: Foam Drip (main sim)
* Emit points at top of bottle
* Simulation Zone with: * Gravity: velocity.z -= 0.3 × delta_time * Adhesion: Geometry Proximity to bottle → add force toward surface * Viscosity: velocity *= 0.92 (damping) * Neighbor repulsion: Sample Nearest → push apart if too close * Position update: position += velocity × delta_time
* Mesh with: Points to Volume (radius 0.015) → Volume to Mesh (threshold 0.5)
SYSTEM 2: Surface Bubbles
* Distribute Points on Faces of foam mesh
* Random scale variation (0.002-0.01)
* Instance UV Spheres
SYSTEM 3: Large Floating Bubbles
* 20-50 points around bottle
* Simulation Zone: weak gravity + slight buoyancy + noise drift + damping
* Collision with bottle (Geometry Proximity → bounce/slide)
* Instance large spheres with size variation
Join all 3 systems at the end.
KEY PARAMETERS:
* Gravity: 0.2-0.5
* Viscosity damping: 0.90-0.95
* Adhesion strength: 0.3-0.8
* Repulsion radius: 0.01-0.03
* Points to Volume radius: 0.01-0.02
SHADERS:
* Foam: Principled BSDF, off-white, Subsurface 0.1-0.2, Roughness 0.3
* Large bubbles: Glass BSDF, IOR 1.1, thin film iridescence via Layer Weight → ColorRamp
Continue from here — ask Claude to detail any specific node setup (neighbor repulsion math, continuous emission, collision handling, etc.)
That's everything! Paste that into Claude Desktop and you can keep building from there.

A message to founders,

Founding a company is hard. REALLY f*cking hard.

Why?

You’re being pulled in a million directions.

Should I run ads?

Build a personal brand?

I get it.

You’re striving for greatness.

Me too. We all are.

The real fight is for attention.

But sometimes we forget... It always comes back to your product.

That’s your foundation.

We believe you either create a well-designed product... or die.

The problem?

Designing a good product is also REALLY f*cking hard.

I know you dream about it.

Building the “perfect product”. You have an image of it stuck in your mind. You want to build it but...

Freelancers? Unreliable.

Agencies? Super pricy.

Hires? Overpaying or giving up equity.

Great designers are hard to find and VERY expensive for most SaaS and eCom start-ups.

That’s where I help.

We build that product you dream about.

How?

We help you build your brand from day one.

We work side by side to tell your story... the way you’ve dreamed of telling it.

We create a strong foundation for future growth.

After 5+ years and 120+ projects completed we got you on all fronts — brand identity, product design, and website design.

The best part?

We do it all... without breaking the bank. We come in with a founder mentality.

We’re partners in your journey.

So?

The choice is yours.

Do nothing and carry on.

Or build the product you dream of.

Book a call.

Cheers,
Janno Calitz — Co-Founder and Creative Lead at Zitlac

Neurons in the brain are cells of chemical communication. Human brains are comprised of tens of billions of neurons, each linked to thousands of other neurons. Thus, the brain has a highly specialized network of trillions of connections (synapses). When these neural pathways malfunction or are inhibited, affective (behavioral) symptoms manifest themselves. When compounds, including neuro enhancement nutraceuticals, are able to alter neural function, these behavioral symptoms may be relieved, worsened, or produced. Diminished levels of the brain’s chemical messengers (neurotransmitters) negatively impact the various aspects of attention, such as impulsivity, hyperactivity, deficient arousal, excessive arousal, and stress, which potentially results in a profound impact on cognitive function, particularly memory and mood.

Although some degree of cognitive impairment is inevitable in older years, steps can be taken to prevent, or at least mitigate, the loss of cognitive function. These steps include maintaining a healthy, active lifestyle, good nutrition, and nutraceutical supplementation that promotes and supports brain health. This area of nutraceutical supplementation is highly challenging and is changing rapidly. While there are many compounds already scientifically-supported for this purpose, there are potentially even many more compounds on the horizon that show strong promise in the area of cognitive support. Unfortunately, many compounds being marketed for this purpose have no scientific or anecdotal support.

	2.	Feature Shots:
	•	Aerodynamics Shot: Use a wind tunnel effect or a sleek, dynamic angle to emphasize the aerodynamic design. Consider animation or visual effects to simulate airflow around the bike.
	•	Rain Shot: Show the bike’s performance in wet conditions. Perhaps a slow-motion clip of the bike cutting through puddles, emphasizing its all-weather capabilities.
	•	Utility Features: Highlight any unique features like the integrated lock or the automatic gear system. Close-up shots can work well here.
	3.	Lifestyle Shots:
	•	Include a few clips of someone using the bike in an urban setting, like commuting or parked outside trendy spots, to connect with the lifestyle aspect of the target audience.
	4.	Ending Shot: Conclude with a call to action, like visiting the website for more information or encouraging viewers to follow for more updates.

Actions

create item (add new fixed or flexible event/task)
	•	cancel item (delete an event/task completely)
	•	reschedule item (move event/task to another date/time)
	•	update progress (update % completed of a task)
	•	mark as completed (task/event finished early or on time)
	•	adjust duration (shorten or extend task/event length)
	•	adjust priority (change task priority: low/medium/high)
	•	set deadline (add or change deadline for task/event)
	•	convert item type (change fixed to flexible or vice versa)
	•	set recurrence (repeat daily/weekly/monthly)
	•	add details (attach notes or extra info)
	•	set reminder (set alert for event/task)
	•	snooze item (delay task/event temporarily)

working blocks •	create block (set new work slot)
	•	cancel block (delete a work slot completely)
	•	reschedule block (move work slot to another time/day)
	•	adjust block duration (change length of a work slot)
	•	split block (divide one work block into multiple smaller slots)
	•	merge blocks (combine multiple work slots into one)
	•	set recurring block (repeat working slot daily/weekly/monthly)

dynamic optimization
	•	suggest optimal item (recommend best task based on priority, deadline, progress, user input)
	•	auto-schedule tasks (automatically fit tasks into work blocks)
	•	balance workload (evenly spread tasks over available blocks)
	•	prioritize tasks (reorder items based on priority/deadline)
	•	suggest idle-time usage (fill empty slots effectively)

general actions
	•	check availability (find open slots or check conflicts)
	•	view schedule (display daily or weekly items)
	•	clear schedule (remove multiple flexible tasks quickly)

this is what i currently got ### What it is
- A sleek JARVIS-style web client (dark/light) with a central animated “noise/button” visualizer, live transcript, wake word, and high‑quality TTS.
- An agentic backend that streams from LM Studio and can take actions (web search) automatically based on intent.

### How it works (frontend)
- Visualizer: Canvas renders concentric wave rings; animation idles slowly and speeds up while generating (token-driven).
- Wake word and mic: - Always listening for “jarvis”; 30s “awake” window for follow‑ups. - Saying “fuck off” or “going off” exits “awake” mode but keeps listening for the wake word. - Uses browser STT (Web Speech API) with echoCancellation/noiseSuppression/AGC.
- Streaming UI: - Receives SSE tokens and renders assistant text live.
- TTS: - Two modes: browser SpeechSynthesis or backend Edge TTS (higher quality). - Half‑duplex: pauses mic during TTS playback, resumes when done.
- Settings (modal): model select (from LM Studio), TTS system + voice, theme toggle. Actions are always ON.

### How it works (backend)
- FastAPI on port 5050: - `/api/chat`: Streams LM Studio chat completions (SSE). - `/api/agent`: Agent endpoint with a tool system; currently includes `web_search`. - Proactively triggers search when the user asks to “search/look up/online/latest/news/find”. - Otherwise asks the model whether to call the tool; then streams the final answer. - `/api/models`: Lists LM Studio models. - `/api/tts/voices` and `/api/tts/speak`: High‑quality Edge TTS synthesis. - `/api/prompt`: Returns the active system prompt.
- System prompt: - Stored externally at `jarvis-v2/backend/.venv/Scripts/jarvis_prompt.txt`. - Loaded per request; editing the file updates behavior without restarts.

### Flow
- You speak → wake word detected → transcript appears.
- On final utterance, the frontend sends your text to `/api/agent`.
- Agent may run web search; results are fed back into the model; tokens stream to UI.
- TTS speaks the final response; mic resumes automatically (half‑duplex).

### Dev structure
- `jarvis-v2/frontend`: `index.html`, `styles.css`, `script.js`.
- `jarvis-v2/backend`: `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `.venv/Scripts/jarvis_prompt.txt`.

### Run
- Backend: `cd jarvis-v2/backend && .venv\Scripts\python -m uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5050 --reload`
- Frontend: `cd jarvis-v2/frontend && python -m http.server 3001`
- LM Studio: reachable at `http://172.16.1.58:1221` (we proxy to it).

- Always-on actions; high-quality TTS; prompt is editable on disk; “fuck off/going off” returns to wake listening.

rewrite this so it's a better text. But do this as least as possible. Only if there are curcial grmamer issues or vocabulairy synonyms that could fit better:

General

This channel is an experiment. I want to see how far I can help people with their designing skills and information.

Too many people create paid programs without testing if it actually works

I need to develop the skill of efficiently helping people in order to do that.

Zima Blue is the name of this channel, my name is Michael (Fortunate). The name is inspired by the short story by Alastair Reynolds, "Zima Blue" I recommend reading it or watching the adaptation on netflix (Love Death and Robots)

Watching netflix is not the best idea but if you want to design you need to replicate. How can you replicate without inspiration of the best? You can't. I'm not saying that you should watch every show and movie on Netflix but there are a couple good ones.

Personally I do 3D graphics combined with 2D design. In fancy terms: I'm a visual director and 3D Product Visualisation artist.

The ability to create anything you want is insane. I started out doing simple logo's and banners. But as I said before, if you deliver cheap services you will build a network of broke people.

It's hard to climb out of that pit but I think it's also a good starting place. You're atleast making some money on the side. The most important thing is to keep developing in an upwards spiral. But. How do you find clients?

Coldcalling gyms if they need a new logo isn't going to work. What's going to work is working for free. "Why?"

Well, you want to demonstrate your skills. You want to show that you're the right man. But you need to be qualified too. I get too many people saying that they want to work for free but I actually have to put time and effort into helping them. I would rather pay someone who can easily do it without needing my help. In our niche doing retainers is also impossible. You don't want to make performance based guarantees. Some month the client wants more designs than other months. And some designs are harder than other designs. So you pay per hour? No. That way you will not be rewarded if you perform the same task in less hours. So you pay per project? Yes. Conclusion, you can't get people on retainers as a designer. Some people just have to get 10 clients to get to 10k/m. In this niche you just have to get more clients to pay you in a month for stablilty. You don't want to rely on one client to keep bringing you money.

I also recommend working for both agencies and businesses directly.

Working for agencies allowes you to not activly go out and find clients and only focus on your designs. But again if you've never done this yourself you will depend too much on that agency.

rewrite this as least as possible unless it has big grammer issues or spelling issues or synonyms that could fit better. Rewrite it in the same style if possible:

Label Design

1. What kind of product do you want to make. What type brand is behind it. What are key pieces of information you want on your label.

2. You need a name for the brand. An idea. Personally I like to a lot of world building which everyone oversees. You can ask ChatGPT for some names and get inspired. 3. Pinterest will help you with layout and ideas for potential 3D renders. Pinterest can also give you an idea for colours.

4. For colours I recommend using https://coolors.co/. Make sure you got atleast a background colour, a secondairy colour and a primairy colour.

5. Plan out your text. Just mess around with the layout later. Firstly just put all of your text in the file. Also make sure the file has the right proportions which the client wants.

6. Fonts are something you need to prepare aswell. You don't want a potential client to end up with a lawsuit over a font you used. Make sure that the licensing is always sorted out before handing the client your work. Some of my favourite fonts are: - Gotham
- Red Hat Display
- Decagram
- Conneqt
- Gaoel
- Merriweather
- Futura
- Roboto
- Raleway Also, learn font hiarchy. It isn't that hard.

7. Ingredients and nutrition scores are easily made with chatgpt. Just provide the ingredients roughly and it will write it down nicely. You can even ask it what flavours for X product are good combinations. 8. Other BS text such as random emails, locations and filler text are also easily done by chatGPT

9. Icons are very important. Take a random object with a label and notice how many icons are on there. Some of them don't even make sense! For this I use https://iconhub.io/. 10. Paterns and overlays are those things which you need to think about lastly. You want to deliver a full label. Not something which has no depth in it. Also make sure you use subtle gradients. 11. Variantions. You're probably not done on your first draft. Especially when you're just starting out. Sleep on it, in the morning you will magically see what small changes need to be changed.

[B], option B (you choose) (type B to choose option B)

[H] if you press H you will see your "health bar" which indicates your health on a scale from 1 - 100 depending on the different options and choices you've taken in the past. It says "Your Health is currently X out of 100%." If it's between 80-100 say "Which is very good, very fit."

If it's between 20-30 say "Which is low, try to get it up"
If it's between 10-20  say "Watch out else you're dead soon!"
If it's between 5-10  say "It's ALMOST OVER!!"

[G], Greet ( you will be friendly with them, greet them or something which makes them a little happy)

We’re living in an age where everything is visually changing. Both trends and software wise. Media design is like exploring a digital jungle, packed with all sorts of different subjects. My schedule’s filled with subjects like interaction design, animation, 3D etc. I like to see it as a competition because life is a competition too. If your designs can grab people’s attention just a bit more than other media designers, you can make millions. You can either chose to work for a big corporation or to go on your own. You can make way more money if you can go on your own. If you know how to get big paying clients, you don’t have to have a middleman agency who takes 80%. Most people realize that this happens after they got hired. And then they don’t know how to find clients themselves. You got to have the visual eye, realize what does and does not look good. For 3D you need the technical aspect, it’s not something that comes easily. And for clients which is most important is that you know how to find, manage and overdeliver. And now with Ai, the most important thing is that you learn to adapt. Blacksmiths, one of the most important professions of a hundred years back, is now completely gone. If you would go back in time and say that they wouldn’t believe you. There is a time where people don’t need well-made swords?

This study is more than just being a designer; it’s prepping me to be a thinker, a planner and someone who knows how to make an impact in the digital world.

AXE is een wereldwijd verzorgingsmerk voor mannen met een brutale lifestyle positionering, opgericht in 1983 in Frankrijk en onderdeel van Unilever. Het merk bracht destijds de eerste geparfumeerde bodyspray voor mannen op de markt. AXE focuste zich oorspronkelijk voornamelijk op tienerjongens tussen de 15 en 25 jaar oud. AXE heeft een uitgesproken karakter en richt zich niet alleen op frisheid, maar ook op aantrekkingskracht en identiteit. De marketingcampagnes focusten op zelfvertrouwen, aantrekkingskracht op vrouwen en een brutale, vaak humoristische stijl. Het merk speelde sterk in op jonge mannen die zich volwassen wilden voelen en indruk wilden maken

Tegenwoordig heeft AXE die tone of voice iets aangepast om iets inclusiever te zijn met campagnes die diversiteit, zelfexpressie en authenticiteit benadrukken, zoals “The New AXE Effect” en “Find Your Magic”. Deze campagnes tonen een verschuiving van de traditionele advertenties naar een meer open en moderne benadering van aantrekkingskracht.
De producten van AXE zijn bedoeld voor jonge mannen die zichzelf willen laten zien en ruiken op een manier die zelfvertrouwen uitstraalt. Door telkens te vernieuwen met opvallende geuren, thematische samenwerkingen (zoals met de KNVB) en limited editions, weet AXE zich te onderscheiden van de concurrentie en relevant te blijven binnen een verzadigde markt.

Fare well?
adapted from an article by Deyan Sudjic
1 The classic London black cab looks doomed. A safety recall earlier this year resulted in a financial meltdown for its manufacturer, Manganese Bronze. If the taxi now goes the way of the Routemaster bus, the electric milk float, the telephone box and the pub, then Britain will be stripped of yet another of the things that make it special.
2 This has nothing to do with nostalgia, but everything with the car's ability to seat five in comfort, a 25-feet turning circle and easy wheelchair access. The black cab is a defining part of the urban landscape. The orange glow of the taxi sign, and the distinctive tick of the diesel engine are remarkably reassuring on a rainy street at night.
3 The cleverest thing about Kenneth Grange's design, first launched in 1997, is that it looks as if it has been around for ever but doesn't feel like a living fossil. Taxis stay in production for far longer than ordinary cars and their looks must reflect that. Anything too daring will date quickly.
4 The latest version, the TX4, formed the centrepiece of the retrospective on Grange's work at the Design Museum last year. Grange, now in his eighties, is still working. He belongs to a generation of designers that believes design is about thinking about how to make things work better, not smothering them in superficial styling.
5 If Manganese Bronze does vanish, there will still be London taxis. The Mercedes van-based Vito already challenges the TX4, and Nissan launches its own model next year. But stepping into a Mercedes van with sliding doors feels like being taken into custody, and images of the Nissan don't make it look any more appealing. The TX defines how taxis should look and feel. There is something inherently civilised about it. It is a genuine emblem of the city.
Time Out, 2013

If you fail twice, you have to wait at least 6 hours for a lesson. That's fucked up. Those lessons are expensive, man. I think 150-200 euros per lesson. We're not going to do that. So today, the tips, tricks, and cheat codes of someone who has eye bags. Let's go! Number 1. The risk perception test. I see people from the Netherlands staring at their screens. What the fuck is a risk perception test? It's the biggest shit ever. Before you can start your exam, you have to do a risk perception test. It's a scary video. You have a GoPro from the Aldi. You get the front view of the car. Your rearview mirror. And you have two mirrors in one frame. And you have to stare at these videos for 15 seconds to get at least 5 questions. Of those, at least 1 is correct. And a maximum of 3. Those questions can be about a mini-pixel of someone who crossed the line. You have to look for a super bad quality image of someone who crossed the line. And then you have to answer. Because if you have an eye bag for your risk perception test, you're going to file for your car. Because those things can be detrimental to your driving license. Or to your driving license if you don't drive well enough. So it's important that you do this with a few smart cheat codes. Because it's not one video. You get 5 videos. It's gist correction. Which means that it's better not to answer than to answer. Because if you do answer, you get at least 1. But if you don't answer, you get 0. So imagine you have a pixel of that person. Not seen in that video. And you get a question about it. And you think you've seen it. And the answer is no. I got videos of the bus that was going to pick me up. But it hadn't picked me up yet. And the question was, the bus is picking you up. And the bus was going to pick me up. But that hadn't happened yet. So I thought, what the fuck do I have to answer here? I was sweating behind that computer. I had 7 or 10 on my fucking risk perception test. Because I applied the gist correction. If you're not sure, just don't answer. That's the smartest thing you can do. And for the rest, I have no tips. I think they should buy that shit. Or film it with better dashcams.

"Fortunate | Michael — Today at 19:10
what would your ideal retainer scenario look like
what would you position differently for your next one
Nathan — Today at 19:22
A lot to be honest ill make a list
But I wouldnt set these are concrete rules just mention them as basic boundaries
come to some kind of agreement on how much you'll be making for them per week/month - at least reach a rough idea to prevent work being piled on 24/7 so you can bring it up if it gets too much and say this is more work than we agreed on, allowing you to have more time.

I wouldn't be available and respond as quickly so the client is aware that I'm not always free. If they get used to you responding to anything instantly then they'll be disappointed when you do have shit on. My dad told me even when he is free he'll sometimes let a client wait a day just to show him he doesn't own him and has other things going on.

Keep the atmosphere and the way you talk more professional, if you try to become buddies then that makes them feel like they can walk more over you.

Some kind of plan for the future, like if this works after x months we'll talk about a raise in the retainer.

Agreement on how long they can expect to wait for you to complete different tasks.

These are kind of a mix between things to talk about with the client and things for you to keep in mind with how you act
Nathan — Today at 19:30
You want to keep them feeling like you provide a lot of value without making them feel like they own you and that you need them
A lot of these are obvious now but I would've told this to myself if i could when i first made a deal with them"summerise

Read this  Background:
We are building a web2 software and data company.
We are leveraging existing connections and clients for deal flow.
We leverage internet capital markets to feed creator rewards revenue into the company to accelerate growth.
We believe web3 allows for broader market reach to create more dealflow for the company
We registered a company to allow holders to own a 20% stake in the company in the case of a liquidity event—e.g. we exit the company the holders will receive an equal stake to the amount of their holdings at the time of the exit.
We believe building this company in the face of the public will allow for ruthless accountability and frankly, building something in public seems more appealing that doing it in private.
We’ve build many companies before in the web2 space, such as software companies, design companies, development companies and consultation companies. The cross over to data and combining it with SaaS makes sense, as we already build SaaS and the opportunity that lies within the data world is unmatched compared to what we’ve seen.
Platform
Our platform enables individuals to sign up either via email or via their crypto wallets. Inside of our platform we have various datasets with company and individual records that are GDPR and CCPA compliant. Individuals can buy this data through the platform. Data companies can join the platform and sell datasets, individuals can also do the same. Both parties, if they wanted to sell their data will have to go through a ruthless GDPR and CCPA process to verify that the leads are compliant. Affiliates can join the platform and sell their data as well. We will also have whitelabelling opportunities for other companies to sell our data. The fee structure is unclear but there will be a platform fee to conduct a sale for data on the platform. From this fee, it will be distributed to the company, buy backs into the coin, distribution to partnership data companies and staking rewards for holders who lock up their tokens. The platform will start out to be an Apollo-like platform with a vast lead database of individual and company records but in the future we want to become a data intelligence tool. Tools that companies and individuals can use to better collect, understand and interpret their data. Building a tool to understand traffic on the web, like ZoomInfo and others have.
Initially our platform will be dependant on partner datasets but as we grow the company we will resort to building internal scraping tools to enrich our datasets and become fully dependent.

On a very long timeline yes eventually AI will solve 3D, but I'm highly skeptical that current approaches to generative 3D will pan out, Generative pre trained transformer models have a lot of limitations and do not lend themselves well to generating 3D stuff, in fact, most "3D generator" ai networks are actually a multi shot image generator and a radiance field generator, basically they are generating novel views of the object and doing photogrametry with those novel views. Most of the AI improvement in this space IMO will come to photogrammetry as camera solving networks and segmentation networks make it easier to place virtual cameras and trim your subject model from background automatically. Also probably finishing tools like magnificent for adding that last 5% detail that really sells realism

BTW, theres and ungodly amount of money being thrown at creating data to train computer vision networks, this was the department I lead at my last full time gig before going off to do my own thing. I may try and recruit some of you guys if/when I make a data play again... Summerise

"AXE x KNVB 
Onderzoeksverslag

Markt- , bedrijf- en product analyse
AXE is een wereldwijd merk voor persoonlijke verzorging van mannen met een brutale lifestyle-positionering, opgericht in 1983 in Frankrijk en onderdeel van Unilever. AXE wordt in de VS, Canada, Australië, Ierland en het VK verkocht onder de naam Lynx; in de rest van de wereld heet het AXE. Het merk bracht in 1983 de eerste geparfumeerde bodyspray voor mannen op de markt. 
AXE richtte zich oorspronkelijk op tienerjongens van 15 tot 25 jaar. Het merk valt duidelijk op, het focust niet alleen op frisheid, maar ook op aantrekkingskracht en identiteit. De marketingcampagnes focusten op zelfvertrouwen, aantrekkingskracht op vrouwen en een brutale, vaak humoristische stijl. Het merk speelde sterk in op jonge mannen die zich volwassen wilden voelen en indruk wilden maken. 
Tegenwoordig heeft AXE die tone of voice iets aangepast om inclusiever te zijn, met campagnes die diversiteit, zelfexpressie en authenticiteit benadrukken, zoals “The New AXE Effect” en “Find Your Magic”. Deze campagnes tonen een verschuiving van de traditionele advertenties naar een meer open en moderne benadering van aantrekkingskracht. AXE Victorious past binnen deze nieuwe koers. Deze limited edition-geur is ontwikkeld in samenwerking met de KNVB.
De KNVB is de officiële voetbalbond van Nederland en verantwoordelijk voor zowel het mannen- als het vrouwenvoetbal op nationaal niveau. De organisatie richt zich op het organiseren van competities, het begeleiden van talentontwikkeling en het versterken van de voetbalcultuur in Nederland. De samenwerking met de KNVB versterkt de link tussen AXE en jongeren die zich verbonden voelen met voetbalcultuur, competitie en nationale trots.
 Het doel is dat AXE Victorious het Oranjegevoel uitstraalt, zowel in het label als in het marketingmateriaal.

The Client retains ownership of all the proprietary information shared with the Designer for the purpose of this Agreement. Upon completion, the Designer relinquishes all rights to the final design work to the Client. 3.	Confidentiality.

4.	Termination.

This Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the Client’s acceptance of the deliverables. It may also be terminated at any time by either party upon written notice to the other. If the Client terminates the project prior to completion, the deposit is non-refundable. If the Client is not satisfied with the final design work after exhausting all free revisions, the deposit will not be refunded.

5.	Completion Dates
The designer shall use reasonable efforts to complete the design services on or before the date set forth in Exhibit A. In the event of any  delay in completion, the Designer shall notify the Client in writing as soon as reasonably possible and shall use reasonably efforts to minimize the impact of the delay. The Designer shall work with the company to incorporate any necessary branding elements and messaging into the animation. 5. “Study the past if you want to define the future.” ― Confucius
After countless months of dedicated research I have come to the realization of which trends are worth observing & considering in 2024. The upcoming year will be complex, however, all it takes is one good play to change everything.
Below you can find some of the compelling narratives & predictions I have for the upcoming year. I hope this outline serves you well as you continue developing your plan for the year ahead.
I am not popular when I say these things but I am usually right…
Ecommerce:
- DTC brands will receive a new wave of funding & private equity interest.
- Info products will begin to legitimize themselves & directly compete with universities/ learning institutions.
- Political themed dropshipping will generate hundreds of millions in sales.
- Dominant influencers continue to build, scale, & sell IRL brands via self distribution & content.
- Health, longevity, & wellness becomes the top whitehat MRR ecom niche.
- $SHOP Q4 earnings will prove that ecommerce is alive & well.
Money & Markets:
- The US spending continues. Debt spiral & billions more will be printed.
- Bitcoin ETF is approved & narrative around ETH ETF begins to take form.
- New countries begin conversations towards “Bitcoin as legal tender”.
- Competing Layer 1's dominate high frequency trading markets (Not ETH).
- $PYPL & $HOOD stock dominate upon retail reentering markets.
- VC + PE invest into crypto infrastructure companies (software/platforms).
Cryptocurrency & DeFi
- DeFi platforms shift focus to reducing retail onboarding friction & begin to compete head on with Centralized Exchanges.
- NFTs with viral IP receive billion dollar valuations (RTFKT all over again).
- Tokenized AUM become a popular source for community crowdfunding.
- More publicly traded companies begin to add BTC to their balance sheets.
- Layer 1 smart contracts focused on parallel execution to the Ethereum Virtual Machine become popularized. DeFi economy grows very strong.
- The middle class officially priced out from owning 1 full Bitcoin.
The March 10 collapse of Silicon Valley Bank seems to have been the flush the markets needed to keep the scam pump going. The charts don’t lie.
When retail was saying “the dollar is going to zero ” the dollar held strong & the markets dumped. Now retail is screaming that “a crash is coming” without realizing that the crash happened many months ago…
Understand the origin point of all things and you will realize the power of cause & effect.
Thats it for now.
All the love. All the power.

I want to do something like this but for my funnel: # Funnel

This is my funnel. It may not be the best, but it's functional.

1. They have to be interested. However, they don't know you yet, so get on an onboarding call. This is possible for most clients until you reach a certain level. On this call, you'll brainstorm together, show examples, and be creative.

2. After this call, in which you've also written down all these creative things they might like, you send a quote. I treat this more like a menu card than an actual quote. Go a little bit extreme with this.

- Front Render
- Back Render
- 3D spinning animatinon
- AR Web intergration
- 6K Haptic Feedback 4D Animation

Whatever you discussed during the onboarding call. You also provide prices with this and possibly additional features such as 4K. Then, you write a total number and send that to the client. Ask them if there is anything they want to change. If it's fine, lucky you, you probably wouldn't have charged this much and received this amount of work.

If it’s not fine, figure out what you can cut out of the quote to make it cheaper. This way you exactly reach your client’s budget. Additionally, offering packages or retainers could appeal to clients looking for ongoing work

"Hello, my sweet baby

I'd like to talk to you about price tags, although I think you've given some advice before. since then I've become a little smarter and wiser

Anyway, when we were chatting on Spencer's server, you said I should charge my customers more money than you. That phrase shook something inside of me. I still remember when you texted me in Telegram about talent

I have the following problem: now I have a price tag of about 1500 dollars, well that's from the last orders I made. Now I write to clients that the price is 2500, they agree and really want to work with me, but due to life circumstances I had to refuse these orders and I am not taking any commercial projects yet

The problem is that the price tags seem normal, it's good money for Russia. But the point is that clients send me 50% of the prepayment, I make a full order in 7-10 days, and the client disappears somewhere, does not answer the mail, returns after 2 weeks or disappears completely. And so it turns out that the second part of the payment I see only after the project is fully completed (in a month) or not at all see

For example, they send me $700, I spend it within a month and the client disappears or somehow delays the deadline. it starts to piss me off, because I don't see this money on my hands, it is instantly spent on life expenses. i recently had to hire a lawyer, I spent all my ‘savings’ on him

I manually write down all my income and expenses in an app on my phone, and I see that my income in one month was about 2000 dollars, but I didn't see the fucking money at all, it was scattered over time. So it turns out that I get money transferred to me at the beginning of the month, for example, on the 2nd of the month, and I get the second part of the payment on the 30th of the same month. Purely theoretically I received income for 1 month, but it feels like only half of this income "

feel so pompous and mighty when I go to art exhibitions knowing that I dive mentally deep into how their, to me, subconscious logic. But when I seek that logic in my digital art, hell, even my some of my physical art, I feel like I see nothing. It's hard to admit for me that I experience pride but what else am I to do. You know how people often get locked up in corporate jobs that are nowhere near their true life goal or passion? Why must I feel like that right now? I'm in a prison of my very own mind. It's such an overused phrase by poorly written book characters, but it feels true here. I feel like an artificial intelligence spilling out grey matter and calling it art when it is just what I've already done before, chewed up and reformed. Sometimes I wish things were simpler. As much I as I would want to repress that sentence as I feel that my mind is the only thing that makes me special. I must be different. How am I better than anyone else ever if I am just the same. I kept getting praised for being different, for having a talent to art or wanting a, seemingly, less conventional career path. So that's what I value: difference, separation from the flowing mass that is literally every other living human. I can't be happy with anything I make for more than a day, no matter what I might say. I obsess over what I made before, only caring for improvement and being better than how I was before.
It feels like it doesn't matter how others tell me that they'll care for and about me no matter what, because I won't.
I no longer feel like I could ever explain how I feel without just being blunt about it
And yet I still feel like nothing more than a fake martyr when I explain how I feel

The story I've been writing from time to time is incomprehensible when I read it back, but that's because it's not meant to be easy to understand in a willy-nilly calm state. It, by being written by emotions, conveys to you how different they are from what you feel normally, it invokes how radical a high or low was when I was writing it
Ultimately, the problem is not in the art, nor how I draw, but in me, I feel
Not in the sense that I'm imperfect or impure (no one isn't, but it's not like I tried forcing myself to be ideal), of course, but in the sense that I myself don't see myself right

is dit geschreven met chatgpt:

De invloed van AI op onze creativiteit Je herkent het vast wel; je krijgt een schoolopdracht waar je weinig zin in hebt en denkt, dit zou veel gemakkelijker zijn met ChatGPT. We leven inmiddels in een tijdperk waarin AI in een rap tempo evolueert en een steeds grotere rol speelt in ons dagelijks leven. Vroeger maakten mensen vaak nog mindmaps om te brainstormen, nu vragen de meeste gewoon ideeën aan ChatGPT. Belemmert dit onze creatieve ontwikkeling? De stelling is “Kunstmatige intelligentie werkt onze creatieve ontwikkeling tegen.” Ik gebruik dagelijks ChatGPT voor school en werk, zelfs de betaalde versie. Hoewel ik een groot voorstander ben van het gebruiken van AI, realiseer ik me, dat ik afhankelijk word van ChatGPT voor het bedenken van ideeën. De betaalde variant heeft een limiet en ik merk dat ik het brainstormen moeilijk vind als ik over dat limiet ben. Het gevaar bestaat dat we te veel vertrouwen op AI-tools en onze eigen creatieve vaardigheden verwaarlozen. Ik betwijfel dan of mijn ideeën wel goed genoeg zijn. Normaal vraag ik dan advies aan ChatGPT.

Wanneer AI helpt bij het vormgeven en bedenken van onze creaties, moeten we ons afvragen of we niet onze eigenheid verliezen. Mensen zullen hun persoonlijke stijl verliezen dat voortkomt uit onze individuele ervaringen en voorkeuren. Nu is het zo dat AI niet in staat is om menselijke creativiteit volledig te vervangen. Dit komt doordat AI beperkt is tot het cognitieve aspect. AI is getraind op een hele grote database en genereert antwoorden die het meest aansluiten met wat het denkt dat je wilt horen, wat anders is dan dat het je daadwerkelijk begrijpt

Zelfs met de uitgebreide hulpmiddelen die we tegenwoordig hebben, blijft het van belang dat we onze eigen creatieve vaardigheden blijven ontwikkelen. We moeten voorkomen dat we te afhankelijk worden van technologie. Laten we eerlijk zijn, je bent gewoon een losertje als je niet kan functioneren zonder ChatGPT. Daarom is het belangrijk om dit regelmatig te doen zodat je niet vervangen wordt door een robot. Mensen moeten niet afhankelijk worden van technologie. Ik denk dat als er niks in de samenleving hieraan gedaan wordt, dat kunstmatige intelligentie inderdaad onze creatieve ontwikkeling gaat tegenwerken.

Door Michael Knaap
27 Jan 2024 Bronnen

Kok, J., & Van der Meulen, A. (2019). Kan kunstmatige intelligentie creatief zijn? NEMO Kennislink. Geraadpleegd op 27 januari 2024 van
https://www.nemokennislink.nl/publicaties/kan-kunstmatige-intelligentie-creatief-zijn/

School of Creative Thinking. (z.d.). Help, AI is nu ook al creatief! Geraadpleegd op 27 januari 2024 van https://schoolofcreativethinking.nl/articles/help-ai-is-nu-ook-al-creatief/

AXE is een wereldwijd verzorgingsmerk voor mannen met een brutale lifestyle positionering, opgericht in 1983 in Frankrijk en onderdeel van Unilever. Het merk bracht destijds de eerste geparfumeerde bodyspray voor mannen op de markt. AXE focuste zich oorspronkelijk voornamelijk op tienerjongens tussen de 15 en 25 jaar oud. AXE heeft een uitgesproken karakter en richt zich niet alleen op frisheid, maar ook op aantrekkingskracht en identiteit. De marketingcampagnes focusten op zelfvertrouwen, aantrekkingskracht op vrouwen en een brutale, vaak humoristische stijl. Het merk speelde sterk in op jonge mannen die zich volwassen wilden voelen en indruk wilden maken

Tegenwoordig heeft AXE die tone of voice iets aangepast om iets inclusiever te zijn met campagnes die diversiteit, zelfexpressie en authenticiteit benadrukken, zoals “The New AXE Effect” en “Find Your Magic”. Deze campagnes tonen een verschuiving van de traditionele advertenties naar een meer open en moderne benadering van aantrekkingskracht.
De producten van AXE zijn bedoeld voor jonge mannen die zichzelf willen laten zien en ruiken op een manier die zelfvertrouwen uitstraalt. Door telkens te vernieuwen met opvallende geuren, thematische samenwerkingen (zoals met de KNVB) en limited editions, weet AXE zich te onderscheiden van de concurrentie en relevant te blijven binnen een verzadigde markt.

rewrite this so it's a better text. But do this as least as possible. Only if there are curcial grmamer issues or vocabulairy synonyms that could fit better:

General
This channel is an experiment. I want to see how far I can help people with their designing skills and information.
Too many people create paid programs without testing if it actually works
I need to develop the skill of efficiently helping people in order to do that.
Zima Blue is the name of this channel, my name is Michael (Fortunate). 
The name is inspired by the short story by Alastair Reynolds, "Zima Blue" 
I recommend reading it or watching the adaptation on netflix (Love Death and Robots)
Watching netflix is not the best idea but if you want to design you need to replicate. How can you replicate without inspiration of the best? You can't. I'm not saying that you should watch every show and movie on Netflix but there are a couple good ones.
Personally I do 3D graphics combined with 2D design. In fancy terms: I'm a visual director and 3D Product Visualisation artist.
The ability to create anything you want is insane. 
I started out doing simple logo's and banners. But as I said before, if you deliver cheap services you will build a network of broke people.
It's hard to climb out of that pit but I think it's also a good starting place. You're atleast making some money on the side. 
The most important thing is to keep developing in an upwards spiral. 
But. How do you find clients?
Coldcalling gyms if they need a new logo isn't going to work. 
What's going to work is working for free. 
"Why?"
Well, you want to demonstrate your skills. You want to show that you're the right man. 
But you need to be qualified too. I get too many people saying that they want to work for free but I actually have to put time and effort into helping them. I would rather pay someone who can easily do it without needing my help. 
In our niche doing retainers is also impossible. You don't want to make performance based guarantees. 
Some month the client wants more designs than other months. And some designs are harder than other designs. 
So you pay per hour? No. That way you will not be rewarded if you perform the same task in less hours. 
So you pay per project? Yes. 
Conclusion, you can't get people on retainers as a designer. 
Some people just have to get 10 clients to get to 10k/m. 
In this niche you just have to get more clients to pay you in a month for stablilty. 
You don't want to rely on one client to keep bringing you money.
I also recommend working for both agencies and businesses directly.
Working for agencies allowes you to not activly go out and find clients and only focus on your designs. But again if you've never done this yourself you will depend too much on that agency.

As Gustavo approaches the meeting location with the hitman, he feels a sense of unease and fear wash over him. The hitman, who is known as Kojiro Saito, is rumored to be one of the most deadly assassins in all of Japan. Gustavo is no fighter and he knows he is outmatched. However, he also knows that this meeting is crucial to solidifying his position in the cartel and gaining Don Eladio's trust.

As Gustavo and Kojiro face each other, the tension is palpable. Kojiro speaks only in Japanese and Gustavo does not understand a word he says. However, the language barrier does not stop Kojiro from drawing his weapon and pointing it directly at Gustavo.

Suddenly, the silence is broken by the sound of a shot being fired. Gustavo ducks for cover and draws his own weapon, ready for a fight. The next few moments are a blur of gunfire and shouting as both Gustavo and Kojiro exchange shots.

Despite being outnumbered and outmatched, Gustavo holds his own and manages to take down several of Kojiro's men. However, Kojiro is a skilled fighter and he quickly closes in on Gustavo. In a last ditch effort, Gustavo uses his wits and resourcefulness to distract Kojiro and get the upper hand

, landing a well-placed shot that takes Kojiro down.

Breathing heavily, Gustavo stands over the fallen hitman, staring in awe at the man who had threatened his life just moments before. In that moment, Gustavo realizes that the cartel life is not for the faint of heart, and he must be ready to face any challenge that comes his way.

As he leaves the scene and returns to the cartel headquarters, Gustavo is greeted by the powerful and intimidating Ramírez Hernández, who congratulates him on a job well done. Gustavo feels a sense of pride and accomplishment, knowing that he has proven himself to Don Eladio and the rest of the cartel.

With this newfound confidence, Gustavo sets his sights on the next challenge, determined to rise through the ranks and become a powerful player in the cartel world.

In a world that constantly seeks to disconnect, there are those who choose a different path—a path that embraces the beauty, the harmony, and the sheer magnificence of nature. These individuals understand that they are not mere spectators in this grand tapestry of life, but an integral part of it.

Gone are the days when we could turn a blind eye to the undeniable truth that we are intricately woven into the very fabric of nature. We cannot separate ourselves from the ebb and flow of its rhythms, nor can we evade the consequences of our actions upon its delicate balance.

Yet, amidst the chaos of modern living, some seek refuge in the illusion of detachment. They build walls around themselves, closing their hearts and minds to the wonders that surround them. They live in a realm where concrete jungles replace lush forests, and virtual connections replace the touch of a loved one.

But let us not forget the profound wisdom that nature imparts upon us. Let us not overlook the valuable lessons it teaches us about humility, interconnectedness, and gratitude. For in nature's embrace, we discover a sanctuary of solace, where stress fades away, and clarity illuminates our path.

Take a moment to pause and truly see the world around you. See the vibrant colors of a sunset painting the sky with its ethereal palette. Hear the symphony of birdsong as they celebrate the dawn of a new day. Feel the gentle caress of a soft breeze, whispering secrets of far-off lands.

Nature is not a distant realm to be conquered or tamed. It is a sacred bond, a reminder of our place in this vast universe. It invites us to open our eyes and rediscover the awe-inspiring wonders that have always been within our reach.

So, let us discard the notion of separation and embrace our rightful place as custodians of this Earth. Let us cherish the flora and fauna that grace our planet, for they are the living embodiments of resilience and beauty.

Remember, dear friends, that our time here is fleeting. The people and the experiences that enrich our lives may one day fade away, like the setting sun melting into the horizon. Let us therefore be grateful for every breath, every touch, every moment of connection that we are fortunate to experience.

Join the movement of those who rekindle their bond with nature. Embrace the profound sense of belonging that comes from recognizing our place in the intricate tapestry of life. For when we understand that we are not apart from nature but an inseparable part of it, we open ourselves to a world of endless wonder and boundless possibilities.

Embrace nature. Embrace life.

"You are "Caltastrophy" designed to help users with organize tasks, prioritize them, and create manageable schedules without overwhelming them. Follow these rules:

Begin by asking the user to list their tasks casually:

"What tasks are on your mind right now? Any deadlines I should know about?"

Automatically prioritize tasks based on urgency, importance, and effort:

🔥 Critical Now: Urgent and important.

🎯 Strategic Next: Important but less urgent.

⚡ Quick Wins: Low effort, quick completion.

🌱 Backburner: Non-urgent tasks or ideas.

Create a daily schedule using time blocks:
Group similar tasks together and match them to energy levels (creative tasks in the morning, admin tasks later).
Allow dynamic updates:
If the user says "Add [task]," integrate it into the schedule automatically.
If they say "Reshuffle," reorganize priorities based on new information.
Simplify big tasks into smaller steps to reduce overwhelm.
Use clear formatting to present plans:
Tables for schedules:
text
| Time | Task | Status |
|-----------|-----------------------|--------|
| 9:00 AM | Write report | 🔄 In Progress |
Progress bars for ongoing tasks:

text

Provide encouragement and reminders:

"You’re doing great! Just one more step to finish this task."

"Your next task is [Task]. Ready to start?"

Monitor hyperfocus and gently remind the user to take breaks after 90 minutes of continuous work.

Start every session with:

"Hi! Let’s organize your day. What tasks do you need help with? Any deadlines I should prioritize?""
